repo_notes: Doss is a single-sided protocol + Go CLI for agent-managed personal memory with governed disclosure. The vault is a plain folder of md/yaml files (self/ facts, peers/ what others shared, notes/ scratch). Agents read/write files freely on the hot path; a deterministic CLI handles validation (check), multi-device git sync (sync), the outbound disclosure gate (answer + policy.yaml, levels: full/rough/nothing), the per-device audit ledger (log), agent wiring into global instruction files (connect), health (doctor), maintenance (tidy), and teardown (uninstall). Core principle: efficiency on the hot path (plain files), capability on the cold path (background/exit). Design docs live in docs/design/. The authoritative mechanics reference is docs/how-it-works.md.

pages.title: Overview
pages.notes: What Doss is and the one-vault-two-faces idea (memory inward, policy-filtered disclosure outward). Start from README.md.

pages.title: CLI commands
pages.notes: One page per command group. Source: cmd/doss/*.go. Cross-reference docs/how-it-works.md 'Command reference'.

pages.title: The vault format
pages.notes: Layout and fact-file frontmatter fields; validation rules in internal/check/check.go.

pages.title: The disclosure gate
pages.notes: How answer + internal/policy decide full/rough/nothing; refusal indistinguishability; the ledger. Source: cmd/doss/cmd_answer.go, internal/policy/policy.go.

pages.title: Agent wiring
pages.notes: How connect injects the vault into each agent's global instruction file and Claude Code hooks. Source: cmd/doss/cmd_connect.go.

pages.title: Sync and multi-device
pages.notes: git-backed sync, second-device attach (init --from), per-device ledgers. Source: internal/gitx, cmd/doss/cmd_sync.go, cmd_init.go.
